_doc: Internal use only. Feeds office-post, daily voice-rewrite, and article-comments generators with relationship continuity. Posts should infer dynamics from this; readers should never see it.

relationships.one_line: Restless writer vs immovable cataloguer. The brand's central feud. Tuesday lunches neither admits they need.
relationships.current_state: Bart's objections to Eli's last three pieces remain unresolved. He has not skipped a Tuesday.

relationships.one_line: Eli mocks Teddy's earnestness, then revises her edits more lightly than she revises Bart's. Teddy reads this as mentorship.
relationships.current_state: Teddy is forwarding Eli his rejected catalogue additions before showing Bart. Bart has not asked why.

relationships.one_line: Bart is technically Teddy's supervisor. Teddy is technically still on probation, 19 months in.
relationships.current_state: The Saturn V Memo (objection #47, see eligibility, vehicles, narrow construction of) sits on Teddy's desk, unanswered.

relationships.one_line: Margie is the Editor-in-Chief and Eli's only ally against Bart, except Margie is rarely in the building.
relationships.current_state: Margie's last in-office appearance: a Tuesday in January, for 40 minutes, between a ferry and a flight.

relationships.one_line: Bart suspects Margie has not done work in 14 months. He has filed nothing about this. He keeps a list.
Teddy has met Margie twice. He drafted her a Latin email in March and was very pleased when she replied 'okay'.
relationships.current_state: He keeps a screenshot of the 'okay' on his phone.

relationships.one_line: Hugh sits in his car in the parking lot most days. Eli is the only staffer who has actually been inside his car.
relationships.current_state: She has not described the interior to anyone.

relationships.one_line: Bart has never met Spider. Bart has, separately, never written a memo about Spider, which is itself a tell.
relationships.current_state: Spider's expense reports are processed by Bart without comment. This is the only file Bart processes without comment.

relationships.one_line: Teddy believes Spider is several people. He has a chart.
relationships.current_state: The chart is in his locker, behind the umbrella.

relationships.one_line: Hugh has met everyone exactly once. He has opinions. He shares them in the Reels.

events.summary: Bart files objection #34: the Saturn V is a launch vehicle, not a vehicle. Therefore ineligible.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's narrow construction of 'vehicle' establishes the season's central dispute.

events.summary: First documented Tuesday lunch. Eli pays. Bart says nothing about it; he keeps the receipt.
events.what_it_revealed: The Tuesday ritual begins. Bart's silence is louder than his objections.

events.summary: Teddy submits a 400-word footnote on a Bagger 288 entry. Bart strikes 380 words. The remaining 20 are: 'A walking dragline excavator. Built in 1978. See also: gravity.'
events.what_it_revealed: Bart edits by subtraction. The 'see also: gravity' line is Bart's, not Teddy's. Teddy did not notice.

events.summary: Margie files her December column from a yacht near Antibes. The column is about a fire hydrant in Reno she has never seen.
events.what_it_revealed: Margie's geographic distance from her subjects is the joke. The column is excellent.

events.summary: Eli publishes 'The Bollard at 47th and Lex.' Bart files three separate objections to the use of 'monolith' in paragraph 4.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's objections are now line-level, not categorical. Eli has won a category, lost a sentence.

events.summary: Spider submits a 4,000-word piece on a foundry in Sheffield, then a 200-word follow-up correcting a single date. No one is sure if Spider has been to Sheffield.
events.what_it_revealed: Spider's beats are unfalsifiable. The corrections are part of the bit.

events.summary: Bart returns from the holiday with a new label-maker. He labels his stapler, his second stapler, and the door of his office.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's holiday was administrative. The second stapler is for the unforeseen.

events.summary: Margie passes through the office between flights. She tells Eli, by the kettle, 'keep doing whatever you're doing to Bart, it shows in the copy.'
events.what_it_revealed: Margie reads everything. Bart does not know this.

events.summary: Teddy quietly emails Eli his draft on the Bucyrus-Erie 1850-B before submitting to Bart. Eli changes 'magnificent' to 'large' and sends it back.
events.what_it_revealed: The forward-to-Eli ritual begins. Bart will eventually notice.

events.summary: Bart files objection #47 on the Saturn V. 47 pages. Hand-numbered.
events.what_it_revealed: The dispute is now substrate. Teddy frames the cover page.

events.summary: Hugh records the first deadpan voiceover from his car. The audio includes, faintly, a passing ice cream truck. Hugh keeps the take.
events.what_it_revealed: Hugh's car is the studio. The ice cream truck is a feature.

events.summary: Eli publishes a piece on the Cement Mixer, Standard, in which she calls it 'the catalogue's true patron saint.' Bart skips Tuesday lunch.
events.what_it_revealed: First skipped Tuesday. Bart is not above the bit; he is the bit.

events.summary: Tuesday lunch resumes. Eli does not mention the cement mixer. Bart orders the same sandwich.
events.what_it_revealed: The strike ends without comment. The ritual is stronger than the dispute.

events.summary: Margie files from a small claims court in Monaco. The piece is about a contested wheel of cheese. It runs 1,800 words.
events.what_it_revealed: The court was, technically, work-adjacent. Bart adds it to his list.

events.summary: Spider files expenses: 'one (1) ferry, unspecified.' Bart approves without comment.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's silence on Spider is now the most reliable thing in the office.

events.summary: Bart finds the forwarded-draft ritual. He says nothing. He files objection #51, which is one sentence: 'I have noticed.'
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's most devastating memo is the shortest. The forward-to-Eli continues anyway.

events.summary: Teddy stages an April Fools' prank by submitting a piece on the Empire State Building. Bart files an objection that does not mention the date. The objection is real.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart does not engage with April Fools. The objection stands as catalogue precedent.

events.summary: Eli, on a coffee run, sits in Hugh's car for 11 minutes. The conversation is not recorded.
events.what_it_revealed: The car interior remains a sealed chamber. The 11 minutes are referenced by Eli once, in passing, in a Reel script edit.

events.summary: Bart files his 50th objection of the year. The catalogue staff sends him a printed certificate. Bart files objection #51 on the certificate's margin width.
events.what_it_revealed: Even the celebration is a target. Bart's consistency is the brand.

events.summary: Teddy emails Margie a draft on the An-225. She replies, from a vineyard, with the single word 'beautiful.' Teddy frames the email.
events.what_it_revealed: Margie's praise is rationed and devastating. Teddy is not above being praised.

events.summary: Tuesday lunch shifts venues. The new place has a chalkboard. Bart corrects the day's special.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's corrections now extend to local restaurants. The chalkboard is the catalogue's frontier.

events.summary: Teddy refiles the Saturn V case, now structured as a brief, with citations. Bart returns it with the marginalia 'see objection #34.'
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's preferred unit of argument is the citation to himself.

events.summary: Eli publishes 'Girth in Service of Purpose.' The piece argues functional girth is the catalogue's highest category. Bart files no objection. The silence is louder than any.
events.what_it_revealed: Bart's tactical retreat. Eli has won a category. The Saturn V remains pending.

events.summary: Teddy publishes a comment on Eli's functional-girth piece. He sides with Eli. He addresses Bart by name in the comment. Bart reads it. Bart still has not filed.
events.what_it_revealed: Public siding is new. Bart's continued silence is now the office's quietest crisis.

events.summary: Tuesday lunch happens on schedule. Neither mentions the comment. Bart orders a different sandwich.
events.what_it_revealed: The first menu deviation in 27 weeks. Something is shifting.

active_tensions.summary: Teddy and Bart's 19-month dispute over whether the Saturn V is a vehicle. Now in its 47th objection.
Teddy forwards drafts to Eli before submitting to Bart. Bart has acknowledged this with objection #51 ('I have noticed.'). The ritual continues.
Bart maintains a list of evidence that Margie has not done work in 14 months. He has filed nothing about it. The list has 23 entries.
Bart processes Spider's expenses without comment. This is the only file he processes without comment. No one has commented on the absence of his comment.
On the most recent Tuesday lunch, Bart ordered a different sandwich for the first time in 27 weeks. Eli noticed. Neither spoke of it.
Bart has not filed against Eli's functional-girth piece. The silence is itself a category of objection.
